response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a capable full-stack and systems developer with a broad portfolio, but he is fundamentally mismatched with this role. The most critical requirement — 8 years of C++ experience — is essentially unmet: C++ does not appear in his skills section and surfaces only in a bachelor thesis project and a 6-month stint writing components for NI LabWindows (Dec 2014 – Jun 2015). His career has been built primarily on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies. He also lacks the cloud compute infrastructure engineering depth (storage, networking, hardware architecture) and the formal technical leadership track record expected at this seniority level.
strengths: Demonstrated exposure to high-throughput distributed systems (Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency DLang apps)
Consistent culture of engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks
Broad polyglot background across embedded, mobile, and backend systems shows adaptability and systems-level thinking
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015 plus a student thesis project) versus the mandatory 8-year requirement — a disqualifying gap
No evidence of cloud compute platform or infrastructure engineering at scale (storage, networking, hardware architecture); AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is limited to a pricing service
No documented technical leadership role: mentoring activities are informal (workshops, dojos) with no evidence of owning large-scope architectural projects or leading engineering teams
